name: Fort DeRussy Beach Park
tagline: Waikiki's widest, least-crowded beach — BBQ grills, calm water, and actual space to put your towel down.
parking: Paid lot off Kalia Rd ($4/hr); street parking on Saratoga Rd if you're lucky
bestTime: Weekday mornings for a near-private beach experience; weekends for BBQ vibes
talkStory: Fort DeRussy was built in 1911 as a coastal defense battery — the concrete bunker that now houses the free U.S. Army Museum once held massive disappearing guns aimed seaward. At 71 acres it is the largest open green space in Waikiki, yet most tourists walk right past. The beach here is wider and less packed than the main Waikiki strip, with the same calm water. BBQ grills and picnic tables make it the only spot in Waikiki where you can actually grill — a move locals have known about for decades.
